steps.title: Fastest path: organize the givens first
steps.content: List the given side lengths and the perimeter first. Then use the triangle perimeter relationship to find the one missing side.

steps.title: Set up the perimeter equation
steps.content:
The perimeter of a triangle equals the sum of its three side lengths. Two sides measure $6$ cm and $9$ cm, and the perimeter is $20$ cm. Let $x$ represent the length of the third side:
$$6 + 9 + x = 20$$

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
$$6 + 9 + x = 20$$
$$15 + x = 20$$
$$x = 5$$
The third side has length $5$ cm. That matches Choice B — 5.
